# Levain — Surdejsbrød App

> En dansk app der hjælper folk med at bage det perfekte surdejsbrød.
> Built by Walther Vindnæs (walthervindnaes).

## Project overview

Levain is a sourdough bread app targeting Danish-speaking users. It consists of:
- A **marketing landing page** (`index.html`) that presents the product and subscription plans
- A **web app page** (`side001.html`) — the main bread recipe generator with sliders for sourness, crustiness, and openness, plus a schedule planner and notifications
- A **payment success page** (`success.html`) shown after a Stripe checkout completes
- A **Node.js/Express backend** (`server.js`) handling Stripe subscriptions — this runs separately and is NOT hosted on GitHub Pages
- **Business logic modules** for a future React Native mobile app

The live GitHub Pages site serves only the static HTML files:
- `https://walthervindnaes.github.io/surdej-app-001/` → `index.html` (landing page)
- `https://walthervindnaes.github.io/surdej-app-001/side001.html` → app page
- `https://walthervindnaes.github.io/surdej-app-001/success.html` → payment success

## Tech stack

- **Frontend (static):** Plain HTML, CSS (inline), vanilla JavaScript — no build step
- **Fonts:** Google Fonts — Playfair Display + DM Sans
- **Backend:** Node.js, Express, Stripe (subscriptions + webhooks)
- **Mobile (future):** React Native (App.js, AppNavigator.js, RecipeScreen.js, StarterCheckScreen.js)
- **Deployment:** GitHub Pages via GitHub Actions (auto-deploys on push to `main`)

## File structure

```
/
├── index.html              # Landing page — marketing site, subscription tiers, login modal
├── side001.html            # Main app — bread recipe generator, schedule, notifications
├── success.html            # Stripe payment success confirmation page
├── server.js               # Express backend — serves static files + Stripe API routes
├── create-checkout.js      # Stripe checkout session helper
├── webhook.js              # Stripe webhook handler
├── package.json            # Node.js dependencies (Express, Stripe, dotenv, cors)
├── breadEngine.js          # Core recipe algorithm — takes sliders (sourness/crustiness/openness) and returns full recipe with ingredients and time schedule
├── breadNotifications.js   # Push notification logic for baking reminders
├── starterAnalysis.js      # Sourdough starter health analysis logic
├── App.js                  # React Native root component
├── AppNavigator.js         # React Native navigation (screens and tabs)
├── RecipeScreen.js         # React Native recipe screen UI
├── StarterCheckScreen.js   # React Native starter health check screen
└── .github/workflows/
    └── deploy.yml          # GitHub Actions — auto-deploys to GitHub Pages on push to main
```

## Key business logic

### Subscription tiers (Stripe)
- **Mini** — 25 kr/month
- **Mellem** — 35 kr/month (14-day free trial)
- **Premium** — 100 kr/month

### Bread recipe engine (`breadEngine.js`)
The `generateRecipe()` function accepts:
- `sourness` (0–100) — controls starter percentage and fermentation time
- `crustiness` (0–100) — controls baking temperature and steam
- `openness` (0–100) — controls hydration level
- `timeMode` — `'quick'` | `'evening'` | `'weekend'`
- `flourGrams` — base flour weight (default 450g)

Returns a full recipe with ingredients, hydration percentages, and a step-by-step time schedule.

## Development

### Run the backend locally
```bash
npm install
cp .env.example .env   # add STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, DOMAIN
npm run dev            # starts nodemon on port 3000
```

### Deploy frontend
Push to `main` — GitHub Actions handles deployment automatically to GitHub Pages.

### Environment variables (backend only)
- `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` — Stripe secret key
- `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` — Stripe webhook signing secret
- `DOMAIN` — Public URL for success/cancel redirects (e.g. https://walthervindnaes.github.io/surdej-app-001)

## Language
The app is written in Danish (da). All UI text, comments in JS files, and variable names in business logic are in Danish.
